Bug in error tracking symbol set bulk deletion introduced by commit 482a58c (authored by oliverb123). The custom ErrorTrackingSymbolSet.delete() method added in commit 482a58c in products/error_tracking/backend/models.py (lines 217-227) wraps S3 object cleanup and unresolved stack frame deletion in a transaction, but the bulk_delete endpoint in products/error_tracking/backend/api/symbol_sets.py at line 115 calls QuerySet.delete() directly, completely bypassing this custom delete() logic from 482a58c. This causes orphaned S3 storage objects and stale stack frames in Postgres every time a user bulk-deletes symbol sets. The single-item destroy endpoint at lines 102-105 correctly calls the instance delete() method from 482a58c. The fix should replace the queryset-level delete() in bulk_delete with per-instance delete() calls, matching the pattern already used in the dagster cleanup job at products/error_tracking/dags/symbol_set_cleanup.py line 63. This is a clear resource leak regression that accumulates orphaned objects over time.
